personas.name: Application Developer
personas.role: Using branching for speculative and experimental work

stories.text: As an application developer, I want to fork a database in constant time so that I can create experimental branches without waiting for data to copy.
As an application developer, I want to merge a branch back into the parent with automatic clean-merge handling so that non-conflicting changes are applied without manual intervention.
As an application developer, I want to choose a conflict resolution policy at merge time so that I can pick the strategy that fits the specific merge scenario.
As an application developer, I want to provide a custom merge function so that I can implement domain-specific conflict resolution logic.

personas.name: Aion Engine
personas.role: Using branching for what-if workflow debugging

stories.text: As the Aion engine, I want to fork a workflow's event history so that I can replay against modified history for what-if debugging without affecting the live workflow.
As the Aion engine, I want to discard a branch without affecting the parent so that failed experiments leave no trace in the live database.
As the Aion engine, I want to merge a successful experimental branch back into the parent so that validated changes can be promoted to the live workflow.
As a debugging developer, I want to name a committed root hash as a snapshot so that I can return to that exact state later for investigation.
As a debugging developer, I want to check out a historical snapshot for read-only access so that I can inspect past state without replaying the entire write history.
As a debugging developer, I want to list all named snapshots with their timestamps so that I can find the right snapshot to investigate.
As a debugging developer, I want to diff two snapshots so that I can see exactly what changed between two points in time.
As an operations engineer, I want to prune old snapshots so that unreferenced tree nodes are reclaimed and disk usage is controlled.
As an operations engineer, I want pruning to report how many nodes and bytes were reclaimed so that I can track the effectiveness of my retention policy.
As an operations engineer, I want pruning to guarantee it never deletes nodes referenced by active branches so that live data is never lost.
As a test environment, I want to fork production state into an isolated branch so that tests run against real data without risking production.
As a test environment, I want the fork to share tree nodes with production so that cloning a large database does not double the storage cost.
